core_mechanism: Constrained energy minimization of a nonlinear field over configurations of fixed topological (winding) charge — i.e. topological solitons — in which adding a mass/penalty term to the energy functional destabilizes the extended, hollow minimizer and drives a morphological transition to a compact, clustered ground state as the conserved charge grows.
brief_reason: The abstract finds minimal-energy multi-soliton configurations at fixed baryon (topological) number by numerically minimizing the field theory's energy, then shows that switching on a pion-mass term makes the shell-like minimizers unstable and collapses them into flat, clustered lower-charge structures.
